tagline: Log Zoom sprint meeting summaries directly to Jira sprints and epics
metaTitle: Zoom + Jira Automation - Sprint Summaries to Jira | GAIA
metaDescription: Connect Zoom and Jira with GAIA. Auto-log Zoom sprint meeting outcomes to Jira, create issues from action items, and keep your Jira board current after every ceremony.
intro:
Sprint ceremonies in Zoom generate commitments, decisions, and blockers that should live in Jira — but when the meeting ends, the notes often stay in someone's notebook or a shared doc that nobody reads. Jira issues go un-updated and sprint boards drift from what was actually discussed.

GAIA closes this gap by processing Zoom meeting summaries and logging outcomes directly to the corresponding Jira sprint. Action items become Jira issues, blockers get flagged, and sprint updates are posted before the team has even closed their laptops.

useCases.title: Create Jira issues from Zoom meeting action items
useCases.description: GAIA parses Zoom call summaries for action items and creates Jira issues in the active sprint. Each issue includes the description, assignee, and due date captured from the meeting.

useCases.title: Post sprint planning outcomes to Jira epics
useCases.description: After a Zoom planning session, GAIA logs the scope decisions and story point commitments as Jira epic comments. Product managers have a clear record of what was committed and why.

useCases.title: Flag Jira blockers surfaced in Zoom standups
useCases.description: Blockers mentioned in a Zoom standup trigger GAIA to set the Blocker flag on the corresponding Jira issue. Scrum masters see impediments updated in real time without manual triage.

useCases.title: Attach Zoom recording to Jira sprint
useCases.description: GAIA adds the Zoom recording URL as a link on the active Jira sprint board. Stakeholders can review any ceremony recording directly from Jira without asking for the link.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and Jira to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and Jira project. Map your recurring Zoom sprint meetings to the corresponding Jira boards and sprints.

howItWorks.step: Configure issue creation and update rules
howItWorks.description: Define which meeting types create new Jira issues, which update existing ones, and how GAIA should infer priority and assignee from meeting context.

howItWorks.step: GAIA logs meeting outcomes to Jira automatically
howItWorks.description: After each Zoom call, GAIA processes the summary and updates Jira accordingly. Sprint boards stay accurate and every commitment is traceable to the meeting where it was made.

faqs.question: Does GAIA work with Jira Software's sprint feature specifically?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA integrates with Jira Software and can add issues to the active sprint, update story status, and post sprint-level comments. It works with both company-managed and team-managed projects.

faqs.question: Can GAIA map different Zoom meetings to different Jira boards?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each recurring Zoom meeting can be mapped to a separate Jira board in GAIA's settings. Frontend standups post to the frontend board; backend planning posts to the backend board.

faqs.question: What happens if Zoom's meeting summary is incomplete?
faqs.answer: If the summary is sparse, GAIA falls back to the raw transcript to extract action items. You can also review GAIA's proposed Jira updates before they are applied if you prefer a human checkpoint.
